title: The Compromise of 1850
description: Test your knowledge of the Compromise of 1850!

assessment_items.question_content:
> “[A]nnexing Texas had meant the addition to the Union of a huge southwestern state where slavery was legal, and slavery’s *spread* was something to which large numbers of Northerners were emotionally and politically opposed. This opposition increased and began to be more strident during the War with Mexico, which many in the North regarded as a war brought on largely by southern expansionists. Hostilities officially ended in February of 1848 with the Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo, as a result of which approximately one-fifth of the United States of 1849 was acquired by the victors. . . .”

>-Source: Holman Hamilton, historian, *Prologue to Conflict,* 1964

**The developments described in the excerpt best illustrate which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the influence of regional identity in politics
the rise of the abolition movement in northern states
the increase in racial conflict after the Mexican-American War
the end of a diplomatic relationship with Mexico

assessment_items.question_content:
> “[A]nnexing Texas had meant the addition to the Union of a huge southwestern state where slavery was legal, and slavery’s *spread* was something to which large numbers of Northerners were emotionally and politically opposed. This opposition increased and began to be more strident during the War with Mexico, which many in the North regarded as a war brought on largely by southern expansionists. Hostilities officially ended in February of 1848 with the Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo, as a result of which approximately one-fifth of the United States of 1849 was acquired by the victors. . . .”

>-Source: Holman Hamilton, historian, *Prologue to Conflict,* 1964

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important political effect of  the annexation of Texas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It led to debates about the extension of slavery to the newly acquired territory.
It led to racial conflicts between Mexicans, Native Americans, and white settlers.
It led to an era of mass migration with the discovery of gold in the state.
It led to the expansion of the United States to the Pacific Ocean.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “We are constantly upbraided for entertaining the wish and intention to extend slavery. This is constantly thrown up to us on every opportunity and in every form, and the cry is raised that the Union is endangered by our course upon this very subject, and this argument is thrown out on every possible occasion that it is we who make these assaults and endanger the permanency of our institutions. It is not so. . . . 

> “I only wished to state that there is danger of the Union; that its foundations are shaken; and that they may probably fall, and I believe that they certainly will fall, if the people who have been dealing these blows so fast and so heavily do not cease the agitation of this subject.”

>-Source: Senator Solomon W. Downs of Louisiana, *The Congressional Globe,* Volume 18, 1849

**The author’s remarks in the excerpt most directly reflected which of the following developments during the mid-nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the increase in sectional conflict
the emergence of a national culture
the growth of the abolition movement
the realignment of political parties

assessment_items.question_content:
> “We are constantly upbraided for entertaining the wish and intention to extend slavery. This is constantly thrown up to us on every opportunity and in every form, and the cry is raised that the Union is endangered by our course upon this very subject, and this argument is thrown out on every possible occasion that it is we who make these assaults and endanger the permanency of our institutions. It is not so. . . . 

> “I only wished to state that there is danger of the Union; that its foundations are shaken; and that they may probably fall, and I believe that they certainly will fall, if the people who have been dealing these blows so fast and so heavily do not cease the agitation of this subject.”

>-Source: Senator Solomon W. Downs of Louisiana, *The Congressional Globe,* Volume 18, 1849

**The excerpt was written in response to:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the proposal of legislation by northern states to ban slavery in all new territory
the passage of legislation by southern states making it illegal to harbor people who escaped enslavement
the extension of citizenship to Mexicans living in the territory ceded by Mexico after the war
the adoption of plans to build a transcontinental railroad across the United States using enslaved labor

assessment_items.question_content:
> “That any attempts by congress to interfere with the institution of slavery in any of the territories of the United States would create just grounds of alarm in many of the States of the union; and that such interference is unnecessary, inexpedient, and in violation of good faith; since, when any such territory applies for admission in to the union as a state, the people thereof alone have the right, and should be left free and unrestrained, to decide such question for themselves.” 

>-Source: State Senator Broderick, *Journal of the California Legislature,* 1850

**The ideas about slavery in the territories expressed in the excerpt are most consistent with which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the principle of popular sovereignty
the belief in Manifest Destiny
the ideas of the Second Great Awakening
the theory of nullification

assessment_items.question_content:
> “That any attempts by congress to interfere with the institution of slavery in any of the territories of the United States would create just grounds of alarm in many of the States of the union; and that such interference is unnecessary, inexpedient, and in violation of good faith; since, when any such territory applies for admission in to the union as a state, the people thereof alone have the right, and should be left free and unrestrained, to decide such question for themselves.” 

>-Source: State Senator Broderick, *Journal of the California Legislature,* 1850

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of debates in the federal government over the extension of slavery
the emergence of the Free Soil Party as a major contender in the election of 1848
the adoption of an antislavery platform by the Democratic Party
the change in population in California following the discovery of gold

**The change depicted on the maps most directly contributed to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the balance of free and slave states in the United States
the sale of acreage in the west to small farmers
the rise in immigration from Germany and Ireland
the end of slavery across the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7fcfe0ab8dcc2209efe07129c10de169.png)

>-Source: [“The United States from 1849 to 1850”](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:United_States_1849-1850.png)

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec2176591019e0e2c73e06846411f3c8.png)

> -Source: [“The United States from 1850 to 1853”](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:United_States_1850-1853-03.png)

**Which of the following most directly contributed to the change over time depicted on the two maps?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the passage of the Compromise of 1850
the construction of the transcontinental railroad
the ratification of the Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo
the increase in  immigration from Asian countries

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Provided that an express and fundamental condition to the acquisition of any territory from the republic of Mexico by the United States, by virtue of any treaty which may be negotiated between them, and to the use by the executive of the moneys herein appropriated, neither slavery nor involuntary servitude shall ever exist in any part of said territory, except for crime whereof the party shall first be duly convicted.”

> -Source: The Wilmot Proviso, 1846

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how national leaders attempted to resolve the issue of slavery in the territories
how the abolition movement gained political support in the 1840s
how sectional tensions waned after the Mexican-American War ended in 1848
how the federal government incentivized mass internal migration to California

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Provided that an express and fundamental condition to the acquisition of any territory from the republic of Mexico by the United States, by virtue of any treaty which may be negotiated between them, and to the use by the executive of the moneys herein appropriated, neither slavery nor involuntary servitude shall ever exist in any part of said territory, except for crime whereof the party shall first be duly convicted.”

> -Source: The Wilmot Proviso, 1846

**Which of the following groups would have been most likely to oppose the policy suggested in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: southern Democrats
northern Whigs
Free-soilers
Abolitionists

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The agitation on the subject of Slavery, now raging through the breadth of the land, presents a most extraordinary spectacle. Congress, after a protracted session of nearly ten months, succeeded in passing a system of measures, which are believed to be just to all parts of the Republic, and ought to be satisfactory to the People. The South has not triumphed over the North, nor has the North achieved a victory over the South. Neither party has made any humiliating concessions to the other.”

> -Source: Senator Stephen A. Douglas, in a speech, 1851

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the passage of the Compromise of 1850
the end of the Mexican-American War
the creation of the reservation system in the West
the sale of land in the Midwest at a low cost

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The agitation on the subject of Slavery, now raging through the breadth of the land, presents a most extraordinary spectacle. Congress, after a protracted session of nearly ten months, succeeded in passing a system of measures, which are believed to be just to all parts of the Republic, and ought to be satisfactory to the People. The South has not triumphed over the North, nor has the North achieved a victory over the South. Neither party has made any humiliating concessions to the other.”

> -Source: Senator Stephen A. Douglas, in a speech, 1851

**According to the excerpt, which of the following best explains the most important effect that the “system of measures” had on the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It preserved the Union and prevented secession by southern states.
It allowed slaveowners to move their enslaved people to free states.
It created a plan for the gradual abolition of slavery across the United States.
It allowed all new states to vote on and decide their status as a free or slave state.
